epic.arc: One human holds a growing autonomous system through a single leveraged stamp. Everything that needs bdo arrives briefed at the scale he steers — epics and their arcs, not tickets — value first, mechanism after, on a surface he can open anywhere. Everything he decides is one line the whole machine obeys, and the system spends his attention like it is the most expensive resource it has, because it is.
epic.context: The doctrine's pulley (§3): a little input from the owner holds a big autonomous load. D-4 makes bdo the last stop on everything, which means the human stage is the system's permanent bottleneck — so its interface quality is not a nicety, it is the throughput of the whole machine. bdo's amendments drive this epic: too-thin tickets (rcp.efd40f7169c8), then ticket-scale itself rejected in favour of epic-scale steering.
epic.value: Your stamp stays leverage as the system grows: you see arcs of value with their context, judge in minutes from wherever you are, and the queue refuses to flood you.
epic.horizon: A served, authenticated page you open on your phone: the whole system's open questions told as stories under their epics, cleared with a thumb, with epic-level verdicts available when an arc should be steered as one unit.

pieces.glue: the briefed surface: every open item told value-first with what each verdict does, rendered to a phone-readable page, cleared through one pen
this directive itself: items file under epics, the inbox briefs the arc before the item, and the owner steers at epic scale
